text:
In the heart of an untouched forest, where the gentle rustling of leaves danced like melody across endless dew-kissed grasslands, a fox sat beside a small brook. His fur was glossy midnight black, shimmering under the dappled sunlight filtering through the branches. He looked quite content.

The fox had just finished a hearty meal of rabbits he'd ambushed. His belly felt comfortably full. Suddenly, he heard a sound—a rustle so quiet it could've been his shadow thinking thoughts.

Curiosity piqued, he crept towards the sound. The rustle evolved into a giggling whisper. That could only be—

A family of small woodland creatures had found their way into his cozy cave. A family of squirrels! They scampered along the cave walls, collecting bits of nut that fell. One particularly nimble tree squirrel paused beside him.

"Good day," it chirred, its cheeks puffing out with excitement.

"It's a good middagestrument
